---
title: "Handbook for MDIG2026 Summer School in Tilburg"
subtitle: "Scripts for processing and analysis"
author: Andrea González Sánchéz Anna Palmann, Babajide Owoyele, Davide Ahmar, James Trujillo, Marijn Hafkamp, Sharjeel Ahmed Shaikh, Šárka Kadavá, Tifanny Matej Hrkalović, Travis Wiltshire, Wim Pouw
date-modified: today
format:
  html:
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-width: 1600  # Adjust the width in pixels
toc: true
toc-depth: 4
toc-expand: 2
toc-title: Contents
jupyter: python3
---

![](images/banner.png){fig-align="center" width=100%}

## Overview

Dear participants,

this handbook collects all scripts associated with the program of MDIG2026 Summer School. 
You will find here introduction of the datasets you will be working in your group projects, scripts that process
these datasets into analyzable time series and features and finally, all scripts associated with the lectures.

For full program and other organizational information, please visit [EnvisionBox website](https://www.envisionbox.org/summerschool2026.html).

The two datasets that we picked for this year are:

- **Balance Corpus** (Li, Yang, Fuchs & Aussems, 2026)

```{=html}

```

[📄 Download the paper (PDF)](Datasets/BalanceCorpus/documentation/LiYangFuchsAussems2026.pdf){target="_blank"}

- **PARSEL dataset** (Hrkalovic, Dudzik, Balliet, Hung, 2024)

Each of these scripts have their own **cheatsheet** to help you get started with the data available for the respective corpus.

```{=html}

```

[📄 Download the paper (PDF)](Datasets/PARSEL/documentation/HrkalovicDudzikBallietHung2024.pdf){target="_blank"}


## Setup

We prepared for you three environments that you can directly install (in Anaconda Prompt) using

```bash

cd Address-of-summerschol-folder
conda env create -f NAME-OF-ENVIRONMENT.yml
```

If you want to work with Envisionhgdetector, use **detector-environment.yml** (summerschool-detector-env)

If you want to work with OpenFace, use **kinematics-environment.yml** (summerschool-kinematics-env)

For all else (continuous acoustics, Opensmile), use **general-environmt.yml** (summerschool-general-env)

Feel free to install new packages to any of your newly created environments using

```bash

conda activate NAME-OF-ENVIRONMENT
pip install PACKAGE-NAME
```
